Tags.description: An optional set of tags describing the schedule.

AssociatedClusterCount.description: The number of clusters associated with the schedule.

AssociatedClusters.description: A list of clusters associated with the schedule. A maximum of 100 clusters is returned.

Grantor.description: The Amazon Web Services account ID of the cluster owner.

Grantee.description: The Amazon Web Services account ID of the grantee of the cluster.

AuthorizeTime.description: The time (UTC) when the authorization was created.

AllowedAllVPCs.description: Indicates whether all VPCs in the grantee account are allowed access to the cluster.

AllowedVPCs.description: The VPCs allowed access to the cluster.

EndpointCount.description: The number of Redshift-managed VPC endpoints created for the authorization.

DatabaseName.description: The name of the database that receives data from the partner.

PartnerName.description: The name of the partner that is authorized to send data.

DataShareArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the datashare that the consumer is to use.

ProducerArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the producer namespace.

AllowPubliclyAccessibleConsumers.description: A value that specifies whether the datashare can be shared to a publicly accessible cluster.

DataShareAssociations.description: A value that specifies when the datashare has an association between producer and data consumers.

ManagedBy.description: The identifier of a datashare to show its managing entity.

DataShareType.description: The type of the datashare created by RegisterNamespace.

DataShareArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the datashare that the consumer is to use.

ProducerArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the producer namespace.

AllowPubliclyAccessibleConsumers.description: A value that specifies whether the datashare can be shared to a publicly accessible cluster.

DataShareAssociations.description: A value that specifies when the datashare has an association between producer and data consumers.

ManagedBy.description: The identifier of a datashare to show its managing entity.

DataShareType.description: The type of the datashare created by RegisterNamespace.

Grantor.description: The Amazon Web Services account ID of the cluster owner.

Grantee.description: The Amazon Web Services account ID of the grantee of the cluster.

AuthorizeTime.description: The time (UTC) when the authorization was created.

AllowedAllVPCs.description: Indicates whether all VPCs in the grantee account are allowed access to the cluster.

AllowedVPCs.description: The VPCs allowed access to the cluster.

EndpointCount.description: The number of Redshift-managed VPC endpoints created for the authorization.

Resources.description: A list of the snapshot identifiers that were deleted.
A list of the snapshots that were modified.

TargetNodeType.description: The node type that the cluster will have after the resize operation is complete.

TargetNumberOfNodes.description: The number of nodes that the cluster will have after the resize operation is complete.

TargetClusterType.description:
The cluster type after the resize operation is complete.

Valid Values: multi-node | single-node

Status.description:
The status of the resize operation.

Valid Values: NONE | IN_PROGRESS | FAILED | SUCCEEDED | CANCELLING

ImportTablesCompleted.description:
The names of tables that have been completely imported .

Valid Values: List of table names.

ImportTablesInProgress.description:
The names of tables that are being currently imported.

Valid Values: List of table names.

ImportTablesNotStarted.description:
The names of tables that have not been yet imported.

Valid Values: List of table names

AvgResizeRateInMegaBytesPerSecond.description: The average rate of the resize operation over the last few minutes, measured in megabytes per second. After the resize operation completes, this value shows the average rate of the entire resize operation.

TotalResizeDataInMegaBytes.description: The estimated total amount of data, in megabytes, on the cluster before the resize operation began.

ProgressInMegaBytes.description: While the resize operation is in progress, this value shows the current amount of data, in megabytes, that has been processed so far. When the resize operation is complete, this value shows the total amount of data, in megabytes, on the cluster, which may be more or less than TotalResizeDataInMegaBytes (the estimated total amount of data before resize).

ElapsedTimeInSeconds.description: The amount of seconds that have elapsed since the resize operation began. After the resize operation completes, this value shows the total actual time, in seconds, for the resize operation.

EstimatedTimeToCompletionInSeconds.description: The estimated time remaining, in seconds, until the resize operation is complete. This value is calculated based on the average resize rate and the estimated amount of data remaining to be processed. Once the resize operation is complete, this value will be 0.

ResizeType.description: An enum with possible values of ClassicResize and ElasticResize. These values describe the type of resize operation being performed.

Message.description: An optional string to provide additional details about the resize action.

TargetEncryptionType.description:
The type of encryption for the cluster after the resize is complete.

Possible values are KMS and None.

DataTransferProgressPercent.description: The percent of data transferred from source cluster to target cluster.

AuthenticationProfileName.description: The name of the authentication profile that was created.

AuthenticationProfileContent.description: The content of the authentication profile in JSON format.

CustomDomainName.description: The custom domain name for the association result.

CustomDomainCertificateArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) for the certificate associated with the custom domain name.

ClusterIdentifier.description: The identifier of the cluster that the custom domain is associated with.

CustomDomainCertExpiryTime.description: The expiration time for the certificate for the custom domain.

ClusterIdentifier.description: The cluster identifier of the cluster associated with the endpoint.

ResourceOwner.description: The Amazon Web Services account ID of the owner of the cluster.

SubnetGroupName.description: The subnet group name where Amazon Redshift chooses to deploy the endpoint.

EndpointCreateTime.description: The time (UTC) that the endpoint was created.

Port.description: The port number on which the cluster accepts incoming connections.

VpcSecurityGroups.description: The security groups associated with the endpoint.

VpcEndpoint.description: The connection endpoint for connecting to an Amazon Redshift cluster through the proxy.

HsmClientCertificate.description: Returns information about an HSM client certificate. The certificate is stored in a secure Hardware Storage Module (HSM), and used by the Amazon Redshift cluster to encrypt data files.

HsmConfiguration.description: Returns information about an HSM configuration, which is an object that describes to Amazon Redshift clusters the information they require to connect to an HSM where they can store database encryption keys.

IntegrationArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the integration.

SourceArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the database used as the source for replication.

TargetArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the Amazon Redshift data warehouse to use as the target for replication.

CreateTime.description: The time (UTC) when the integration was created.

KMSKeyId.description: The Key Management Service (KMS) key identifier for the key used to encrypt the integration.

AdditionalEncryptionContext.description: The encryption context for the integration. For more information, see Encryption context in the Amazon Web Services Key Management Service Developer Guide.

Tags.description: The list of tags associated with the integration.

RedshiftIdcApplication.description: Contains properties for the Redshift IDC application.

TargetAction.description:
A JSON format string of the Amazon Redshift API operation with input parameters.

"{\"ResizeCluster\":{\"NodeType\":\"ra3.4xlarge\",\"ClusterIdentifier\":\"my-test-cluster\",\"NumberOfNodes\":3}}".

Schedule.description:
The schedule for a one-time (at format) or recurring (cron format) scheduled action. Schedule invocations must be separated by at least one hour.

Format of at expressions is "at(yyyy-mm-ddThh:mm:ss)". For example, "at(2016-03-04T17:27:00)".

Format of cron expressions is "cron(Minutes Hours Day-of-month Month Day-of-week Year)". For example, "cron(0 10 ? * MON *)". For more information, see Cron Expressions in the Amazon CloudWatch Events User Guide.

IamRole.description: The IAM role to assume to run the scheduled action. This IAM role must have permission to run the Amazon Redshift API operation in the scheduled action. This IAM role must allow the Amazon Redshift scheduler (Principal scheduler.redshift.amazonaws.com) to assume permissions on your behalf. For more information about the IAM role to use with the Amazon Redshift scheduler, see Using Identity-Based Policies for Amazon Redshift in the Amazon Redshift Cluster Management Guide.

State.description: The state of the scheduled action. For example, DISABLED.

NextInvocations.description: List of times when the scheduled action will run.

StartTime.description: The start time in UTC when the schedule is active. Before this time, the scheduled action does not trigger.

EndTime.description: The end time in UTC when the schedule is no longer active. After this time, the scheduled action does not trigger.

SnapshotCopyGrant.description:
The snapshot copy grant that grants Amazon Redshift permission to encrypt copied snapshots with the specified encrypted symmetric key from Amazon Web Services KMS in the destination region.

For more information about managing snapshot copy grants, go to Amazon Redshift Database Encryption in the Amazon Redshift Cluster Management Guide.

ClusterIdentifier.description: The identifier of the cluster with a usage limit.

FeatureType.description: The Amazon Redshift feature to which the limit applies.

LimitType.description: The type of limit. Depending on the feature type, this can be based on a time duration or data size.

Amount.description: The limit amount. If time-based, this amount is in minutes. If data-based, this amount is in terabytes (TB).

Period.description: The time period that the amount applies to. A weekly period begins on Sunday. The default is monthly.

BreachAction.description:
The action that Amazon Redshift takes when the limit is reached. Possible values are:

  • log - To log an event in a system table. The default is log.

  • emit-metric - To emit CloudWatch metrics.

  • disable - To disable the feature until the next usage period begins.

DataShareArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the datashare that the consumer is to use.

ProducerArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the producer namespace.

AllowPubliclyAccessibleConsumers.description: A value that specifies whether the datashare can be shared to a publicly accessible cluster.

DataShareAssociations.description: A value that specifies when the datashare has an association between producer and data consumers.

ManagedBy.description: The identifier of a datashare to show its managing entity.

DataShareType.description: The type of the datashare created by RegisterNamespace.

AuthenticationProfileName.description: The name of the authentication profile that was deleted.

ResourceOwner.description: The Amazon Web Services account ID of the owner of the cluster.

SubnetGroupName.description: The subnet group name where Amazon Redshift chooses to deploy the endpoint.

EndpointCreateTime.description: The time (UTC) that the endpoint was created.

Port.description: The port number on which the cluster accepts incoming connections.

VpcSecurityGroups.description: The security groups associated with the endpoint.

VpcEndpoint.description: The connection endpoint for connecting to an Amazon Redshift cluster through the proxy.

SourceArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the database used as the source for replication.

TargetArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the Amazon Redshift data warehouse to use as the target for replication.

CreateTime.description: The time (UTC) when the integration was created.

KMSKeyId.description: The Key Management Service (KMS) key identifier for the key used to encrypt the integration.

AdditionalEncryptionContext.description: The encryption context for the integration. For more information, see Encryption context in the Amazon Web Services Key Management Service Developer Guide.

Tags.description: The list of tags associated with the integration.

DatabaseName.description: The name of the database that receives data from the partner.

PartnerName.description: The name of the partner that is authorized to send data.

Status.description: The registration status of the cluster or serverless namespace.

LoggingEnabled.description: true if logging is on, false if logging is off.

BucketName.description: The name of the S3 bucket where the log files are stored.

S3KeyPrefix.description: The prefix applied to the log file names.

LastSuccessfulDeliveryTime.description: The last time that logs were delivered.

LastFailureTime.description: The last time when logs failed to be delivered.

LastFailureMessage.description: The message indicating that logs failed to be delivered.

LogDestinationType.description: The log destination type. An enum with possible values of s3 and cloudwatch.

LogExports.description: The collection of exported log types. Possible values are connectionlog, useractivitylog, and userlog.

AquaConfiguration.description: This parameter is retired. Amazon Redshift automatically determines whether to use AQUA (Advanced Query Accelerator).

AuthenticationProfileName.description: The name of the authentication profile that was replaced.

AuthenticationProfileContent.description: The updated content of the authentication profile in JSON format.

ParameterGroupName.description: The name of the cluster parameter group.

ParameterGroupStatus.description: The status of the parameter group. For example, if you made a change to a parameter group name-value pair, then the change could be pending a reboot of an associated cluster.

CustomDomainName.description: The custom domain name associated with the result for the changed custom domain association.

CustomDomainCertificateArn.description: The certificate Amazon Resource Name (ARN) associated with the result for the changed custom domain association.

ClusterIdentifier.description: The identifier of the cluster associated with the result for the changed custom domain association.

CustomDomainCertExpiryTime.description: The certificate expiration time associated with the result for the changed custom domain association.

ResourceOwner.description: The Amazon Web Services account ID of the owner of the cluster.

Port.description: The port number on which the cluster accepts incoming connections.

TargetArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the Amazon Redshift data warehouse to use as the target for replication.

KMSKeyId.description: The Key Management Service (KMS) key identifier for the key used to encrypt the integration.

AdditionalEncryptionContext.description: The encryption context for the integration. For more information, see Encryption context in the Amazon Web Services Key Management Service Developer Guide.

Tags.description: The list of tags associated with the integration.

ClusterIdentifier.description: The unique identifier of the cluster associated with this lakehouse configuration.

LakehouseIdcApplicationArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the IAM Identity Center application used for enabling Amazon Web Services IAM Identity Center trusted identity propagation on a cluster enabled with Amazon Redshift federated permissions.

LakehouseRegistrationStatus.description: The current status of the lakehouse registration. Indicates whether the cluster is successfully registered with the lakehouse.

CatalogArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the Glue data catalog associated with the lakehouse configuration.

RedshiftIdcApplication.description: Contains properties for the Redshift IDC application.

TargetAction.description:
A JSON format string of the Amazon Redshift API operation with input parameters.

"{\"ResizeCluster\":{\"NodeType\":\"ra3.4xlarge\",\"ClusterIdentifier\":\"my-test-cluster\",\"NumberOfNodes\":3}}".

Schedule.description:
The schedule for a one-time (at format) or recurring (cron format) scheduled action. Schedule invocations must be separated by at least one hour.

Format of at expressions is "at(yyyy-mm-ddThh:mm:ss)". For example, "at(2016-03-04T17:27:00)".

Format of cron expressions is "cron(Minutes Hours Day-of-month Month Day-of-week Year)". For example, "cron(0 10 ? * MON *)". For more information, see Cron Expressions in the Amazon CloudWatch Events User Guide.

IamRole.description: The IAM role to assume to run the scheduled action. This IAM role must have permission to run the Amazon Redshift API operation in the scheduled action. This IAM role must allow the Amazon Redshift scheduler (Principal scheduler.redshift.amazonaws.com) to assume permissions on your behalf. For more information about the IAM role to use with the Amazon Redshift scheduler, see Using Identity-Based Policies for Amazon Redshift in the Amazon Redshift Cluster Management Guide.

State.description: The state of the scheduled action. For example, DISABLED.

NextInvocations.description: List of times when the scheduled action will run.

StartTime.description: The start time in UTC when the schedule is active. Before this time, the scheduled action does not trigger.

EndTime.description: The end time in UTC when the schedule is no longer active. After this time, the scheduled action does not trigger.

Tags.description: An optional set of tags describing the schedule.

AssociatedClusterCount.description: The number of clusters associated with the schedule.

AssociatedClusters.description: A list of clusters associated with the schedule. A maximum of 100 clusters is returned.

ClusterIdentifier.description: The identifier of the cluster with a usage limit.

FeatureType.description: The Amazon Redshift feature to which the limit applies.

LimitType.description: The type of limit. Depending on the feature type, this can be based on a time duration or data size.

Amount.description: The limit amount. If time-based, this amount is in minutes. If data-based, this amount is in terabytes (TB).

Period.description: The time period that the amount applies to. A weekly period begins on Sunday. The default is monthly.

BreachAction.description:
The action that Amazon Redshift takes when the limit is reached. Possible values are:

  • log - To log an event in a system table. The default is log.

  • emit-metric - To emit CloudWatch metrics.

  • disable - To disable the feature until the next usage period begins.

ReservedNode.description: Describes a reserved node. You can call the DescribeReservedNodeOfferings API to obtain the available reserved node offerings.

ResourcePolicy.description: The content of the updated resource policy.

Status.description: The registration status of the cluster or serverless namespace.

ParameterGroupStatus.description: The status of the parameter group. For example, if you made a change to a parameter group name-value pair, then the change could be pending a reboot of an associated cluster.
